Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root which represents a core meaning or concept, however ط°ظڈظˆ and its nine plural and feminine forms are built on a fixed form

ط°ظڈظˆ is a masculine, singular, demonstrative pronoun meaning with, on, in, of and it is the root of dhawan ط°ظژظˆظژظ†

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing ط°ظڈظˆ and its plural and feminine forms

Form 8 is the reflexive or middle voice (ظ…ظڈط·ظژط§ظˆظگط¹ظŒ) of form 1. The reflex object is either a) the direct object or accusative or b) the indirect object or dative implying for oneself or for oneâ€™s own advantage. Reflexive verbs describe actions that a subject or subjects perform on itself or themselves using reflexive pronouns like myself, ourselves, yourself, him or herself, itself etc

2) It is common in this form to express reciprocity (describing actions that two or more people perform on each other)

3) Occasionally the original reflexive meaning passes into the passive, especially in verbs which do not have form 7

4) In a few verbs form 8 and form 1 agree so closely that they may be translated by the same word

A word in the Arabic language will either be 1) a verb or 2) a noun or 3) a particle â€“ ظ„ظ‘ظڈط¨ظژط¯ظ‹ط§ is a noun

A noun may either be 1) Definite or indefinite 2) Masculine or feminine 3) Singular, dual or plural 4) Nominative, accusative or genitive

ظ„ظڈط¨ظژط¯ظŒ is the natural state of this indefinite noun and it becomes ظ„ظڈط¨ظژط¯ظ‹ط§ due to it being the: 1) ط§ظ„ظ…ظژظپظ’ط¹ظڈظˆظ’ظ„ظڈ ط¨ظگظ‡ظگ object of a verb or 2) ط§ط³ظ’ظ…ظڈ of ط¥ظگظ†ظ‘ظژ or 3) ط®ظژط¨ظژط±ظڈ of ظƒظژط§ظ†ظژ or 4) ط§ظ„ظ…ظژظپظ’ط¹ظڈظˆظ’ظ„ظڈ ظپظگظٹظ’ظ‡ظگ adverb of time or place or 5) ط§ظ„ظ…ظژظپظ’ط¹ظڈظˆظ’ظ„ظڈ ظ„ظژظ‡ظڈ noun that gives the reason for doing a thing or 6) ط§ظ„ظ…ظژظپظ’ط¹ظڈظˆظ’ظ„ظڈ ظ…ظژط¹ظژظ‡ظڈ noun that comes after the ظˆ which means â€œalong withâ€‌ or 7) ط§ظ„ظ…ظژظپظ’ط¹ظڈظˆظ’ظ„ظڈ ط§ظ„ظ…ظڈط·ظ’ظ„ظژظ‚ظڈ is the ظ…ظژطµظ’ط¯ظژط±ظŒ of the verb occurring in the sentence or 8) ط§ظ„ط­ظژط§ظ„ظڈ adverb of manner or 9) ط§ظ„طھظ‘ظژظ…ظ’ظٹظگظٹظ’ط²ظڈ noun that specifies the meaning of a vague word or 10) ط§ظ„ظ…ظڈط³ظ’طھظژط«ظ’ظ†ظژظ‰ noun that comes after ط¥ظگظ„ظ‘ظژط§ meaning â€œexceptâ€‌ or 11) ط§ظ„ظ…ظڈظ†ظژط§ط¯ظژظ‰ noun of calling or 12) itâ€™s a noun of dependent declension a) ظ†ظژط¹ظ’طھظŒ / طµظگظپظژط·ظŒ adjectives b) ط§ظ„طھظ‘ظژظˆظ’ظƒظگظٹظ’ط¯ظڈ emphatic c) ط§ظ„ظ…ظژط¹ظ’ط·ظڈظˆظ’ظپظŒ conjunction d) ط§ظ„ط¨ظژط¯ظژظ„ظڈ synonym

Active and passive particles indicate on an action or occurence whereas the resembling particle indicates on an attribute

Most of these adjectives come from intransitive verbs ط§ظ„ط£ظژظپظ’ط¹ظژط§ظ„ظڈ ط§ظ„ظ‘ظژط§ط²ظگظ…ظژط©ظڈ / verbs that express a state or condition or signify an act which is confined to the subject, and express mostly, a quality inherent and permanent in a person or thing and partly a certain degree of intensity

Adjectives which are made like the participlesآ آ /آ  ط§ظ„طµظ‘ظگظپظژط§طھظڈ ط§ظ„ظ…ظڈط´ظژط¨ظ‘ظژظ‡ظژط©ظڈ

Active and passive particles indicate on an action or occurence whereas the resembling particle indicates on an attribute

Most of these adjectives come from intransitive verbs ط§ظ„ط£ظژظپظ’ط¹ظژط§ظ„ظڈ ط§ظ„ظ‘ظژط§ط²ظگظ…ظژط©ظڈ / verbs that express a state or condition or signify an act which is confined to the subject, and express mostly, a quality inherent and permanent in a person or thing and partly a certain degree of intensity

The form ظپظژط¹ظڈظ„ظŒ is primarily derived from the verb form ظپظژط¹ظڈظ„ظژ

ظ„ظژط§ is

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root which represents a core meaning or concept, however the ظ„ظژط§آ is built on a fixed form

ظ„ظژط§ is an adverb of negation and a negative particle meaning no, not at all, do not, not (with nouns and verbs) – negative ظ„ظژط§

ظ„ظژط§ is also used to deny the existence of a thing , equivalent to ظ„ظژظٹظ’ط³ظژ, generally governs the accusative – ظ„ظژط§ negating the genus

ظ„ظژط§ is also used as a negative imperative – prohibitive ظ„ظژط§آ 

ظ„ظژط§ is also used to draw pointed attention to the subject which is about to be introduced – attention ظ„ظژط§

ط·ظژط؛ظ’ظˆظژط§

A word in the Arabic language will either be 1) a verb or 2) a noun or 3) a particle â€“ ط·ظژط؛ظ’ظˆظژط§ is a noun

A noun may either be 1) Definite or indefinite 2) Masculine or feminine 3) Singular, dual or plural 4) Nominative, accusative or genitive

A word in the Arabic language will either be 1) a verb or 2) a noun or 3) a particle â€“ ط«ظژظ…ظڈظˆظ’ط¯ظڈ is a noun

A noun may either be 1) Definite or indefinite 2) Masculine or feminine 3) Singular, dual or plural 4) Nominative, accusative or genitive

ط«ظژظ…ظڈظˆظ’ط¯ظڈ is the natural state of this definite noun (Proper nouns in Arabic are known by convention and through the fact that they have the grammatical property of being definite even though they do not carry the al- determiner prefix) and it is in this state ( ظ…ظژط±ظ’ظپظڈظˆظ’ط¹ظŒ) when the noun is: 1) the subject ط§ظ„ظ…ظڈط¨ظ’طھظژط¯ظژط£ظڈ or 2) the predicate ط§ظ„ط®ظژط¨ظژط±ظڈ or 3) ط§ط³ظ’ظ…ظڈ ظƒظژط§ظ†ظژ or 4) ط®ظژط¨ظژط±ظڈ ط¥ظگظ†ظ‘ظژ or 5) ط§ظ„ظپظژط§ط¹ظگظ„ظڈ subject of a verb in the active voice or 6) ظ†ظژط§ط¦ظگط¨ظڈ ط§ظ„ظپظژط§ط¹ظگظ„ظگ subject of a verb in the passive voice or 7) itâ€™s a noun of dependent declension a) ظ†ظژط¹ظ’طھظŒ / طµظگظپظژط·ظŒ adjectives b) ط§ظ„طھظ‘ظژظˆظ’ظƒظگظٹظ’ط¯ظڈ emphatic c) ط§ظ„ظ…ظژط¹ظ’ط·ظڈظˆظ’ظپظŒ conjunction d) ط§ظ„ط¨ظژط¯ظژظ„ظڈ synonym

Name of an ancient tribe which lived in the western parts of Arabia. Thamud is known after a grandson of Aram, the grandson of Noah. They lived shortly before the time of Ismail. Their Prophet was Salih. The tribe flourished more then two hundred years after â€کAd and their territory extended from Adan in the south to Syria in the north.آ
